title: Kanban board with drag-drop card reorder and WIP limits
business_context: Project board: cards drag between columns. WIP limit per column blocks new drops. 20 collaborators per board. Scale: 50k boards.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG with column + card position. Advisory lock per column for reorder.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE columns(id BIGSERIAL PK,board_id BIGINT,name TEXT,position INT,wip_limit INT);\nCREATE TABLE cards(id BIGSERIAL PK,column_id BIGINT,position NUMERIC,title TEXT,version INT DEFAULT 1);\nCREATE INDEX ON cards(column_id,position);
database_design.indexes: (column_id,position) for ordered fetch; numeric position allows fractional inserts.
database_design.queries: Move card: tx with column lock + WIP check + position update.

race_conditions_addressed: Same position race: fractional positions sidestep; uniqueness not required.
WIP race: lock + check inside tx.
Card deleted mid-drag: UPDATE returns 0 rows; ack 'no_card'.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-column lock fine; periodic position rebalance when fractions explode.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client recomputes order. WIP enforcement bypassable.

title: Slack-style threads with rich-text and code blocks
business_context: Rich-text chat: bold/code/lists. Code highlighted server-side. Threads. Scale: standard.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Server parses + sanitizes markdown; highlight code blocks; cache by content hash.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE rt_messages(id BIGSERIAL PK,channel_id BIGINT,sender_id BIGINT,body_md TEXT,body_html TEXT,thread_id BIGINT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nCREATE TABLE highlight_cache(hash TEXT PK,html TEXT);

race_conditions_addressed: XSS: server-side DOMPurify sanitization.
Highlight crash: try/catch falls back to raw.
scaling_considerations: 10x: highlight cache by hash; cap message body to 16KB.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: dangerouslySetInnerHTML client-side. XSS waiting.

title: Live KPI scoreboard for sales team with leaderboard
business_context: Sales team gamification: live $ booked per rep, top 10 leaderboard. Scale: 1k reps.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo time-series for bookings; aggregation pipeline for leaderboard.

database_design.schema: const BookingSchema=new Schema({_id:String,repId:String,amountCents:Number,bookedAt:Date,cancelled:Boolean});\nBookingSchema.index({repId:1,bookedAt:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Cancellation: filter excludes cancelled.
Rep removed: stays in historical aggregation.
scaling_considerations: 10x: pre-aggregate hourly buckets; query buckets not raw.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: counter integer per rep. Cancellation = drift.

title: Pictionary-style: drawer streams strokes, guessers see+type
business_context: Drawing party game. 1 drawer, 6 guessers. Drawer's strokes stream live; guesser types answers; correct answer auto-detected. Scale: 5k rooms.
failure_modes_to_handle: Cheating via DOM inspect (drawer must hide word)
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for room + rounds. Strokes ephemeral in Redis.

database_design.schema: const PictRoomSchema=new Schema({_id:String,players:[String],currentRound:{drawerId:String,word:String,wordMasked:String,startedAt:Date,winnerId:String,guesses:[{userId:String,text:String,at:Date}]},status:String});

race_conditions_addressed: Multiple correct same instant: findOneAndUpdate winnerId:null guard ensures only first wins.
Drawer can't guess: server check.
Word hidden from guessers: server returns masked word in join ack.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: send word to all. Word visible in client devtools.

title: Sealed-bid auction with reveal phase
business_context: Sealed-bid (Vickrey-style): bids hidden until deadline; then revealed and second-price applied. Scale: 1k auctions.
constraints: Server can verify but not see plaintext until reveal
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for commitments + reveals. Commitment = hash(bid + nonce).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE sealed_auctions(id BIGSERIAL PK,closes_at TIMESTAMPTZ,reveals_at TIMESTAMPTZ,winner_id BIGINT,second_price_cents BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE sealed_bids(auction_id BIGINT,bidder_id BIGINT,commitment TEXT,amount_cents BIGINT,nonce TEXT,submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),revealed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(auction_id,bidder_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Late reveal: not revealed = not counted; bidder loses.
Mismatch: server rejects.
Tie: ORDER BY amount DESC + bidder_id ASC deterministic.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: send plaintext bid. Server sees and could front-run.

title: University class roll-call with QR check-in and seat plan
business_context: In-class attendance: students scan QR; live seat plan shows who's there. Scale: 5k classes/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for classes + check-ins. Mongo for seat plans (varied per room).

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE classes(id BIGSERIAL PK,course_id BIGINT,starts_at TIMESTAMPTZ,room_id BIGINT,current_qr_token TEXT,qr_rotated_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE TABLE checkins(class_id BIGINT,student_id BIGINT,seat TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),qr_token TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(class_id,student_id));\nconst SeatPlanSchema=new Schema({_id:String,roomId:String,seats:[{id:String,x:Number,y:Number}]});

race_conditions_addressed: QR replay: rotation invalidates old tokens.
Wrong seat: server allows (audit later); could enforce.
Duplicate scan: PK guard.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: static QR. Students share to absent friends.

title: Video timestamped notes with live transcript scrubbing
business_context: Educational video review: students add notes at specific timestamps; teacher sees aggregate. Scale: 10k videos.
constraints: Per-user notes private; aggregate public density
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for notes. Aggregation for density.

database_design.schema: const VideoNoteSchema=new Schema({_id:String,videoId:String,authorId:String,t:Number,body:String,private:Boolean,createdAt:Date});\nVideoNoteSchema.index({videoId:1,t:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Privacy: server filter on broadcast.
Concurrent edits: separate notes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client-side filter. Private notes leak.

title: Granular Slack-style mention modes (channel vs DM)
business_context: Per-channel notification override: 'all messages' vs 'mentions only' vs 'mute'. DM always notify. Scale: 1M users.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for prefs per (user, channel) + workspace default.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE channel_prefs(user_id BIGINT,channel_id BIGINT,mode TEXT,snooze_until TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(user_id,channel_id));\nCREATE TABLE workspace_defaults(workspace_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,mode TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(workspace_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Override race: UPSERT atomic.
Snooze end: dispatch re-checks at fire time.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: local prefs. Sync broken across devices.

title: Smart traffic light coordination across intersection grid
business_context: City traffic: 1k intersections each with phase state; coordinator algorithm syncs green waves. Live operator dashboard. Scale: 1k intersections.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for intersection state. Mongo for traffic flow time-series.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE intersections(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,current_phase TEXT,phase_started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,manual_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT false);\nCREATE TABLE phase_log(id BIGSERIAL PK,intersection_id BIGINT,phase TEXT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ,reason TEXT);\nconst FlowSchema=new Schema({intersectionId:String,ts:Date,vehicles:Number,direction:String},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'intersectionId'}});

race_conditions_addressed: Override race: manual_override flag prevents coordinator changes.
Cascading flash: per-intersection independent.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: in-memory state. Crash = lost phase.

title: Office hours queue: tutor on standby, student joins WebRTC on next
business_context: Tutoring platform: students queue; tutor sees queue; pops top student into WebRTC. Scale: 500 tutors.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for queue + sessions.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE office_queue(id BIGSERIAL PK,tutor_id BIGINT,student_id BIGINT,joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),status TEXT DEFAULT 'waiting');\nCREATE INDEX ON office_queue(tutor_id,status,joined_at);
database_design.queries: FIFO pop: SELECT ORDER BY joined_at LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.

race_conditions_addressed: Pop while student cancel: SKIP LOCKED + status update; cancel sees 'active' → too late.
Tutor disconnect: queue persists; new tutor session resumes.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: in-memory queue. Tutor disconnect = lost queue.

title: OKR planning workshop with live aspiration scoring
business_context: OKR session: team votes on key results' aspiration (0-1). Live aggregate. Scale: 500 sessions.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for sessions + KRs + votes.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE okr_sessions(id BIGSERIAL PK,team_id BIGINT,quorum INT);\nCREATE TABLE okr_krs(id BIGSERIAL PK,session_id BIGINT,text TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE okr_votes(kr_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,score NUMERIC,anonymous BOOLEAN,at TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(kr_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Spam: UPSERT idempotent per user.
Anonymous leak: emit aggregate only, never individual; PG votes stored with anonymous flag.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: append vote on each click. Drift + duplicates.

title: Long-running test suite with per-test status streaming
business_context: CI test runner: 5000 tests; UI shows live pass/fail per test. Scale: 100 simul builds.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for build + test status. Mongo for failure stacktraces.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE builds(id BIGSERIAL PK,commit_sha TEXT,status TEXT,total INT,passed INT DEFAULT 0,failed INT DEFAULT 0,skipped INT DEFAULT 0);\nCREATE TABLE test_results(build_id BIGINT,name TEXT,status TEXT,duration_ms INT,attempt INT,PRIMARY KEY(build_id,name,attempt));\nconst FailureSchema=new Schema({buildId:String,testName:String,stack:String,attempt:Number});

race_conditions_addressed: Worker crash: per-test result persisted; resume from last reported.
Retry: attempt counter increment.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-test row write rate; batch inserts.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: emit on every test, no persist. Crash = lost results.

title: PRD doc with section-level @comments and resolved tracking
business_context: Product spec collaborative review: section comments, resolve, suggested edits. Scale: 30k docs.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for comments anchored to (docId, sectionId).

database_design.schema: const PrdCommentSchema=new Schema({_id:String,docId:String,sectionId:String,authorId:String,body:String,replies:[{authorId:String,body:String,at:Date}],resolved:Boolean,resolvedBy:String,createdAt:Date});\nPrdCommentSchema.index({docId:1,sectionId:1,resolved:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent resolve: guard.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: comments embedded in doc JSON. Doc edit = comment lost.

title: Anonymous community chat with shadowban + audit trail
business_context: Anonymous discussion forum: posts shown to all but mods can shadowban (poster sees own posts, others don't). Scale: 100k DAU.
constraints: No IP tracking visible to non-mods
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for shadowbans + mod log. Mongo for posts.

database_design.schema: const PostSchema=new Schema({_id:String,authorId:String,body:String,ts:Date});\nCREATE TABLE shadowbans(user_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,banned_by BIGINT,banned_at TIMESTAMPTZ,reason TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE mod_log(id BIGSERIAL PK,action TEXT,target_user BIGINT,mod_id BIGINT,reason TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());
database_design.queries: Filter on emit: if poster shadowbanned, emit only to self.

race_conditions_addressed: Two mods ban: UPSERT idempotent.
Shadow leak: server-side filter only.
Cache invalidation: Redis del on ban.
scaling_considerations: 10x: cache shadowban set per user; per-region invalidation.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client-side hide. Shadowbanned user sees their post missing → realizes.

title: Stock portfolio live P&L per holding
business_context: Investor app: portfolio refreshes P&L as quotes tick. Scale: 100k users.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for holdings. Quote stream from external feed via worker.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE holdings(user_id BIGINT,symbol TEXT,qty NUMERIC,cost_basis_cents BIGINT,currency TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(user_id,symbol));\nCREATE TABLE quotes(symbol TEXT PRIMARY KEY,price_cents BIGINT,updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ);

race_conditions_addressed: Holding edit mid-stream: subscription set refreshed on next subscribe.
Stale rate: timestamp + UI badge.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-user filter at fan-out scales linearly; sharded by symbol hash to limit per-node fan.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: refetch portfolio every second. DB scan storm.

title: Poker table state with per-player private card view
business_context: Online poker: 6 seats; per-player hole cards private; community cards public. Scale: 10k tables.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for tables + hands + actions.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE poker_tables(id BIGSERIAL PK,seats JSONB,current_hand_id BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE hands(id BIGSERIAL PK,table_id BIGINT,community_cards TEXT[],pot_cents BIGINT,current_seat INT,deadline TIMESTAMPTZ,actions JSONB DEFAULT '[]');\nCREATE TABLE hole_cards(hand_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,cards TEXT[],PRIMARY KEY(hand_id,user_id));
database_design.queries: Per-user hole fetch from hole_cards on seat.

race_conditions_addressed: Two actions: lock + seat check.
Auto-fold: deadline checker treats inaction as fold.
Disconnect: state preserved; player auto-folds at deadline.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: broadcast all cards. Cheaters see hole cards.

title: Charity fundraiser thermometer with anonymous + named donations
business_context: Live fundraiser: thermometer rises as donations stream. Donor names scroll (opt-in anonymous). Goal alert. Scale: 100 simul events.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for donations (financial). Redis counter for hot total.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE charity_events(id BIGSERIAL PK,goal_cents BIGINT,total_cents BIGINT DEFAULT 0,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE donations(id BIGSERIAL PK,event_id BIGINT,donor_id BIGINT,name_at_donation TEXT,amount_cents BIGINT,anonymous BOOLEAN,refunded BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent donations: PG UPDATE atomic.
Refund: separate tx subtract.
Anonymous: server-side name swap on emit.
scaling_considerations: Per-event PG total — hot row but tolerable.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: count in JS memory. Crash = thermometer reset.

title: Movie watch-party: synced playback + chat
business_context: Watch party: 1 host plays, viewers see same scene. Pause/seek synced. Chat. Scale: 1k parties.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for party state (timestamp + playing/paused).

database_design.schema: const PartySchema=new Schema({_id:String,videoId:String,hostId:String,currentTime:Number,playing:Boolean,updatedAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Drift on join: server compensates by elapsed time.
Host disconnect: state persisted; new host election (not shown).
Slow viewer: client drops frames to catch up.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client clock sync. Drift across viewers.

title: Music sheet rehearsal markup with per-instrument layer
business_context: Orchestra rehearsal: conductor marks tempo/dynamics; each musician sees their part + own markings + conductor's. Scale: 200 orchestras.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for markings with instrument scoping. PG for orchestra membership.

database_design.schema: const MarkSchema=new Schema({_id:String,scoreId:String,instrumentId:String,measureIdx:Number,kind:String,payload:Schema.Types.Mixed,authorRole:String,deletedAt:Date});\nMarkSchema.index({scoreId:1,instrumentId:1});\nCREATE TABLE orchestra_members(orchestra_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,instrument_id BIGINT,PRIMARY KEY(orchestra_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Layer leakage: server-side per-socket instrumentId filter on emit.
Part reassignment: on next join, fresh instrument id.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client filter. Other musicians see flute markings on violin part.

title: Multi-channel customer journey orchestration (drip campaign)
business_context: Marketing automation: customer enters funnel; sequence of emails/SMS/in-app over weeks. Scale: 5M customers in flight.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for journey state. Mongo for templates. BullMQ for delayed jobs.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE journeys(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,steps JSONB);\nCREATE TABLE journey_state(user_id BIGINT,journey_id BIGINT,current_step INT,paused_until TIMESTAMPTZ,paused BOOLEAN,unsubscribed BOOLEAN,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(user_id,journey_id));\nCREATE TABLE journey_history(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,journey_id BIGINT,step INT,action TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Unsubscribe mid-flight: worker checks unsubscribed flag at start.
Branch change: re-evaluated per step.
Failed step: BullMQ retry; if hard fail, advance with audit.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: cron job. No state isolation, hard to pause individual users.

title: Smart-meter aggregation by utility for grid load
business_context: Utility company aggregates kW across all customers in real time for grid load forecasting. Per-customer privacy. Scale: 1M meters.
architecture_reasoning: 1. TimescaleDB hypertable + continuous aggregates per region.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE meter_samples(meter_id BIGINT,customer_id BIGINT,region_id BIGINT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ,kw NUMERIC,PRIMARY KEY(meter_id,ts));\nSELECT create_hypertable('meter_samples','ts');\nCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW region_1m WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS SELECT region_id, time_bucket('1 minute',ts) AS bucket, sum(kw) AS total FROM meter_samples GROUP BY region_id, bucket;

race_conditions_addressed: Late arrival: continuous aggregate refreshes lazily, picks up.
Privacy: only aggregate exposed.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: per-meter aggregation in app. 1M queries/sec dies.

title: Telemedicine triage queue with priority and live wait time
business_context: Telehealth: patients queue; nurse triages by symptom severity; doctor pops by priority. Live wait estimate. Scale: 1k clinics.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for queue. Mongo for triage notes.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE telehealth_queue(id BIGSERIAL PK,clinic_id BIGINT,patient_id BIGINT,priority INT DEFAULT 5,triage_notes TEXT,status TEXT,joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),popped_by BIGINT,popped_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE INDEX ON telehealth_queue(clinic_id,status,priority,joined_at);
database_design.queries: Pop: ORDER BY priority ASC, joined_at ASC.

race_conditions_addressed: Pop race: SKIP LOCKED.
Cancel mid-pop: rare; doctor sees no patient → retry.
Priority recompute: index uses (priority,joined_at).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: FIFO. Critical patients wait.

title: Audit checklist for SOX compliance with sign-offs in order
business_context: SOX audit checklist: 100 items, multiple auditors with role-based sign-offs (preparer/reviewer/approver). Scale: 5k audits/quarter.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for checklist + sign-offs + audit log.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE audits(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE audit_items(id BIGSERIAL PK,audit_id BIGINT,description TEXT,evidence_url TEXT,prep_sign_by BIGINT,prep_sign_at TIMESTAMPTZ,rev_sign_by BIGINT,rev_sign_at TIMESTAMPTZ,app_sign_by BIGINT,app_sign_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE TABLE audit_log(id BIGSERIAL PK,audit_id BIGINT,item_id BIGINT,actor_id BIGINT,action TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Out-of-role sign: sequence check inside lock.
Reopen race: separate op clears all sign columns + audit log.
Bulk: client iterates; partial fail returns errors per item.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client orders sign clicks. Regulator: not provable.

title: Streaming compliance report generation
business_context: Compliance report: aggregates 6 months of transactions; renders to PDF. Streams progress + final URL. Scale: 100 simul.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for txn data. Mongo for cached report metadata.

database_design.schema: const ReportSchema=new Schema({_id:String,paramsHash:String,status:String,pages:Number,rendered:Number,storageKey:String,createdAt:Date});\nReportSchema.index({paramsHash:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Repeat request: paramsHash dedup.
Cancel mid: per-page check.
Storage fail: status='failed', retry.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: synchronous request. 10min wait, no progress, timeout.

title: Capstone: collaborative IDE with multi-language LSP backend
business_context: Cloud IDE: 5 developers edit project; LSP server runs server-side; diagnostics broadcast. Scale: 1k workspaces.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for files + edits log. PG for workspace + container assignment.\n2. LSP runs per workspace in dedicated process; diagnostics piped via socket.
race_conditions_addressed: Save conflict: version check.
LSP crash: bridge auto-restart per workspace.
Diagnostic burst: client throttles render.
scaling_considerations: 10x: per-workspace container; LSP isolation prevents cross-tenant impact.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: shared LSP for all. Cross-tenant code visible; LSP crash takes everyone down.
